description: Normal pressure hydrocephalus — classic triad (gait apraxia, urinary incontinence, cognitive decline). A reversible cause that must not be dismissed as Alzheimer's.

input.exam: magnetic/shuffling gait, urinary incontinence, mild cognitive slowing

transcript.text: Over the past year my walking has gotten worse — my feet feel stuck to the floor — and I've started having accidents with my bladder.
How about your thinking or memory?
I'm a little slower and more forgetful, but it's the walking and bladder that bother me most.
